artifacts.expected_text: Did you know? The hamburger wasn't actually invented in Hamburg, Germany.
Did you know? The hamburger wasn't actually invented in Hamburg, Germany.
The first hamburger bun wasn't introduced until the 1904 World's Fair in St. Louis — before that, burgers were eaten with bread slices.
The first hamburger bun wasn't introduced until the 1904 World's Fair in St. Louis — before that, burgers were eaten with bread slices.

Did you know Allied soldiers called any enemy tank a Tiger — regardless of its actual type — because the Tiger I was so feared? The American M4 Sherman was produced over 49,000 times — winning through sheer quantity over German armor quality. The Soviet T-34 shocked German engineers so much they considered copying its sloped armor for their own future tank designs.

Germany's Panzer 8 Maus weighed 188 tonnes — so heavy it could only cross rivers by driving along the submerged riverbed.
